title: Inflammation-mediated fibroblast activation and immune dysregulation in Col7a1 deficient skin
author: Morgan Anderson-Crannage
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-3.0-us
description: Through single-cell RNA sequencing and profiling of dynamic immune responses in  col7a1  deficient mice (animal models of recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa), Anderson-Crannage and Ascensión et al. identify a non-cancerous dermal microenvironment exhibiting similarities to various tumor microenvironments. They reveal a unique inflammatory cancer- associated fibroblast (iCAF)-like subpopulation and highlight the role of inflammation in tissue priming.
steps_raw: Through single-cell RNA sequencing and profiling of dynamic immune responses in  col7a1  deficient mice (animal models of recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa), Anderson-Crannage and Ascensión et al. identify a non-cancerous dermal microenvironment exhibiting similarities to various tumor microenvironments. They reveal a unique inflammatory cancer- associated fibroblast (iCAF)-like subpopulation and highlight the role of inflammation in tissue priming.
